item.title: Intense.
item.description: Interesting how some people put in just one drop, while others pour in as much as they can to push the next person closer to losing.
item.dc:creator: ADegenFromUpcountry

item.title: The Best News of Last Week - April 3, 2022
item.description:
1. House passes bill to cap cost of insulin at $35 a month - The House passed a bill capping the cost of insulin at $35 a month in a move that could impact diabetics around the country. McBath, Rep. Daniel Kildee and Rep. Joseph Morelle, co-sponsored the Affordable Insulin Now Act in the House.
2. Sacred land returned to Native tribe in Virginia. More than 460 acres of land will be reacquisitioned - Tribal land in Virginia was returned to the Rappahannock Tribe during a celebration hosted by the Department of the Interior Friday.
3. All 42 children who were reported missing on school trip were found - 42 children were reported missing on Monday during a school trip in Nahal Og near the Dead Sea. All were located and are in good condition, rescue forces announced at 8:30 p.m.
4. EU wants to force fashion firms to make clothes more durable and recyclable - The European Commission is calling for an end to fast fashion by 2030, as it announced a vast expansion of eco-design rules that could in future apply to any product, starting with textiles.
5. It's a girl: Super rare Sumatran rhino born in captive-breeding center - Indonesia has reported the birth of a Sumatran rhinoceros in a captive-breeding program targeted to save the critically endangered species from extinction. The new calf is the first child of captive rhino Rosa at the Way Kambas Sumatran Rhino Sanctuary, and Andatu, a male who was himself born at the sanctuary in 2012.
6. Airbus flew its A380 on 100% sustainable aviation fuel made from used cooking oil - As per a statement on Airbus' website, the first A380 flight fueled by 100% Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) took place on Friday. Airbus' A380 test plane took off from Blagnac Airport in Toulouse, France at 08:43 on March 25th, according to the firm.
7. Kids wrote pleas to help unwanted shelter pups find homes. It worked. - Second-grade students at St. Michael's Episcopal School in Richmond, as part of a class project, wrote persuasive letters to help get animals at Richmond Animal Care and Control adopted.
That's it for this week :) Until next week, you can read my previous posts on my blog here https://feelgoodnews.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: FeelGoodNews

item.title: Gonna be 38 this month and finally gonna make a living wage
item.description: 42k/yr, paid vacations, work from home, mon-fri no weekends. I'm psyched. PSYCHED! FP Edit: Somehow this got to front page? What do I do?! I WASN'T PREPARED FOR THIS!
item.dc:creator: HighSorcerer

item.title: One Good Apple Tried
item.description:
https://twitter.com/nowthisnews/status/1510188538725384192
She rushed in when her supervisor seemed to really lose control. No officers appeared to help her either pull him away from the suspect, or get the supervisor under control. The supervisor was put on administrative leave—with pay—and is under criminal investigation.
https://wsvn.com/news/local/commission-meeting-looking-into-sunrise-police-sergeant-who-grabbed-female-officer-by-throat-turns-heated/ Good luck to the intervening officer.
item.dc:creator: RedNightHawk

item.title: Anatolian Pup in Training - Hard Working Boi
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: Sand Witch Chronicles #1 - Cucumber Tea
item.description:
I've been on this site for years in shadows - no comments, no posts, no account. Now, almost a year after finally making an account, I've finally decided to attack a personal bucket list challenge o' mine. I found this list of sandwiches on a dump ages ago and saved it without thinking. I guess I had always told myself "Wouldn't it be fun to try and make all of these?" There are forty sandwiches in total, so with forty weeks left in the year...let's find out.
I'm going to start from the bottom and work my way up. How fitting that I begin this journey with misery encased in bread...
It's gonna be pretty literal with the ingredients here, so don't expect to much this time around. Also, I couldn't find a banana, so here's a scale for scale. First things first: the cucumber.
This sandwich is an iconic snack in many parts of the United Kingdom. True to its name, Cumber Teas are served during high tea (an afternoon meal between lunch and dinner). That's about all the research I'm willing to put into this mess. Onto the butter.
It was this exact moment I began to question whether this sandwich might've been too much for me to handle - I completely forgot to thaw the butter. No worries, patience is a virgin. And since I am in absolutely no hurry to devour cucumbers and unsalted butter, wait for optimal thaw I shall!
And no, that's NOT a hair...
See?
To be truly accurate, I'm slicing off the crust. I'm starting to feel like a mother from days of old.
I really didn't think today would be the day I'd sink low enough to eat unsalted, buttered crusts and enjoy it, but here we are.
Behold! A mesmerizing array of green goodness!
And case closed! Just one last step before we're done.
Voila! I even got it to look like the photo!
So, how does it taste? Honestly, it doesn't really taste period. The cucumbers added some nice texture, but have no flavour whatsoever, and the less-than-salted butter didn't fair much better. I will say though that I enjoyed eating it. Whether it's because it tasted better than I thought it would, or simply because I made it myself, I couldn't tell you. I'm still trying to make peace with the fact that I may have preferred the buttered crusts more, but I wouldn't mind trying this one out again in the future.
As stated before, this is served with tea, so I jovially brewed up some authentic, rustic tea leaves in an OBNOXIOUSLY YELLOW ICED TEA CARTON I FORGOT TO MOVE OUT OF FRAME...........just for you.
Since, I have no other sandwich to compare it with yet, I'll be giving this abomination 1st place. Feel free to comment any tips on ways to improve this sandwich. Thanks for reading and hopefully I'll see you next week! EDIT: Whoa!! Front page! I never imagined this would blow up so fast. I'm so happy to see all of your support and advice for recipes in the future. I've added some tags for ease of anyone who wants to follow. Thank you Imgur community, see you next Saturday!
item.dc:creator: MrSwissroll

item.title: Artworks by Johannes Voss

1 Gallia of the Endless Dance
2 Tyvar Kell
3 Angel of Vitality
4 Saprolings
5 Monarch
6 Vivien's Invocation
7 Rosethorn Acolyte
8 Spirit
9 Bloodmad Vampire
10 Guard Captain Milandross [Ruze adv]
11 Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
12 Knight
13 Stoneforge Mystic
14 Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
15 Survivor For more of Johannes art, see https://www.artstation.com/algenpfleger

item.title: Day 309: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: The Avenue Burger 50/50 Blend Of Chorizo & Beef Patty, Caramelized Onions, Lettuce, Tomato, Fried Egg, Guacamole, Cheddar & Pepper Jack Cheese, Very Hot Three-Pepper Aioli From Barrelhouse 101 In Ventura, California Thank You @PubstarHero For Recommending This Spot! 12/10 Previous Day 308: https://imgur.com/gallery/JHcCqt2 (April Fools) MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD
